title: Secrets, GITHUB_TOKEN, Variables, Permissions, and Injection Defense
description: A workflow interpolates an issue title into shell, grants write-all globally, exposes a secret to an untrusted build, prints a derived credential, and mistakes masking for access control. Learners cumulatively produce workflow authority and data-flow policy through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Classify public configuration, non-secret variables, sensitive values, credentials, OIDC identity, and derived secrets by authority and lifetime.
Set default no or read authority and grant job-scoped GITHUB_TOKEN permissions only for exact API mutations.
Pass untrusted context through environment or structured input, quote at the receiving language, validate shape, and avoid generated shell or code.
